name: Hiranyakashipu

visual_attributes.morphology: Humanoid with regal and imposing stature
distinctive_features: Golden throne often associated with him
Anthill and vegetation enveloping his body during penance
Skeletal form due to extreme austerities
visual_attributes.texture: Rough and earthy due to anthill and vegetation
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Larger than average human, towering presence
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Regal posture, often depicted seated on a throne

domain_specific_visual.AURA: Blazing fire emanating from his head during penance

functional_attributes.primary_function: Ruler of the Asuras and antagonist to Vishnu

domain_specific_functional.ROLE_IN_MYTH: Symbol of arrogance and defiance against divine order
domain_specific_functional.NARRATIVE_FUNCTION: Catalyst for the appearance of Narasimha avatar

relationships.description: Hiranyaksha was slain by the Varaha avatar of Vishnu, leading to Hiranyakashipu's enmity with Vishnu.
Prahlada was a devout follower of Vishnu, much to Hiranyakashipu's dismay.
Hiranyakashipu sought to kill Vishnu due to his brother's death.
Hiranyakashipu was killed by the Narasimha avatar of Vishnu.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Mythological realms of the three worlds
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the dangers of unchecked ambition and defiance against divine will

contrastive_constraints: NOT without the context of his penance and austerities
